name: Trimble Construction & Geospatial API
what_it_does: Trimble REST APIs for construction, geospatial, and transportation technology platform. Enables AI agents to manage construction project data from Trimble Connect BIM collaboration platform, handle GPS and geospatial positioning data from Trimble GNSS receivers and field controllers, access Tekla Structures BIM model data for structural engineering applications, retrieve field data collection from construction survey and layout workflows, manage transportation route optimization and fleet telematics data from Trimble TMS, handle geospatial data processing and point cloud integration from Trimble Reality Capture, access construction takeoff and estimation data from Trimble Estimation, retrieve field activity and crew productivity data from construction workforce management, manage subcontractor and owner portal collaboration for construction projects, and integrate AEC, geospatial, and transportation data across Trimble product ecosystem.
use_cases: Accessing BIM project data from construction coordination agents
Retrieving GPS positioning data from field surveying agents
Managing transportation routes from logistics optimization agents
Analyzing construction productivity from project management agents
not_for: Enterprise ERP without construction and geospatial domain focus
Consumer mapping without precision GNSS and professional survey context
Non-AEC industries without construction, survey, or transportation operations
best_when: A construction firm, surveyor, or transportation company using Trimble products wants AI agents to automate BIM coordination, GPS data integration, transportation routing, and field productivity analytics.
avoid_when: SAFETY RISK: Geospatial positioning data used for machine control (grading, excavation) and construction layout requires survey-grade accuracy validation; automated layout from inaccurate GPS data causes construction errors with safety and cost consequences. Transportation route automation must comply with HOS and carrier regulations.

auth.notes: Trimble uses OAuth 2.0 with Trimble Identity platform. Developer portal at developer.trimble.com. Multi-product API ecosystem: Trimble Connect (BIM), Tekla (structures), TMS (transportation). Open-source components on GitHub (trimble-oss). Webhooks for project and data events. Sunnyvale, California HQ.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Trimble Connect developer access free; product-specific APIs require active software license
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual software subscription per product (Tekla, TMS, etc.); API access included with license
pricing.notes: Sunnyvale, California. Founded 1978. Public (TRMB NASDAQ). $3.7B+ annual revenue. Construction, geospatial, and transportation technology. 28,000+ employees. Strong in construction (Tekla, Viewpoint), geospatial (GNSS, survey), and transportation (TMS, fleet). Competes with Autodesk and Hexagon for AEC and geospatial markets.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; documented per product API

known_agent_gotchas: SAFETY RISK: GPS machine control and construction layout data requires survey-grade accuracy validation; automated layout from degraded GPS signals causes construction errors
Multi-product API fragmentation — Trimble has dozens of products with separate APIs (Connect, Tekla, TMS, survey); each product has its own endpoint, auth scope, and data model
Tekla Structures API — Tekla's primary API is .NET-based (Tekla Open API); REST API has limited coverage; full BIM model manipulation requires .NET SDK
License-gated API access — most Trimble product APIs require active software license; verify license entitlement before building integrations
Trimble Identity multi-product — single Trimble Identity OAuth covers multiple products but scopes are product-specific; token scope mismatch causes silent access denial
Transportation HOS compliance — Trimble TMS route optimization must incorporate driver Hours of Service regulations; automated routing without HOS constraints creates legal liability
agent_readiness.security_notes: Construction, geospatial, and transportation. SOC2, GDPR. OAuth2. US/EU. BIM, GPS positioning, and transportation data.

security_score_components.security_notes: AEC and geospatial. SOC2, GDPR. OAuth2. US/EU. BIM, GPS, and construction project data.
